pi_items.source: Task setup_task.sh and export_result.sh both reference this path

pi_items.verified_value: All four are real drugs: Methotrexate (cancer/autoimmune drug), Ibuprofen (NSAID), Diclofenac (NSAID), Paracetamol/Acetaminophen (analgesic)
pi_items.source: Medical/pharmaceutical databases (Drugs.com, PubMed, WHO ATC classification)

Multiple authoritative sources confirm: Drugs.com, Patient.info, PubMed, NHS guidance, Arthritis Foundation all state that methotrexate + ibuprofen increases methotrexate toxicity risk due to reduced renal clearance, potentially causing bone marrow suppression, kidney damage, severe GI ulcers, and documented fatal cases

Multiple authoritative sources confirm: Drugs.com, PubMed, PMC, GoodRx. Diclofenac (NSAID) reduces methotrexate elimination by inhibiting renal tubular secretion. Documented fatal interaction case: elderly patient on weekly methotrexate 20mg + diclofenac developed renal failure and pancytopenia and died. Cochrane review confirms increased risk of serious adverse events

Patient.info, Cochrane systematic review (PMID: 22942332), BMC Pharmacology and Toxicology. Paracetamol is much safer than NSAIDs with methotrexate. Cochrane review explicitly notes: 'For paracetamol, no studies were identified for inclusion' regarding methotrexate safety. Clinical consensus is paracetamol is acceptable but hepatotoxicity monitoring recommended due to shared liver metabolism. No serious adverse event signal detected

pi_items.verified_value: Cannot independently verify exact color without accessing app; however consistent with high-risk NSAID classification
pi_items.source: Cancer Drug Interactions system uses traffic light colors (red/amber/orange/yellow/green) to indicate severity. Ibuprofen-methotrexate is a dangerous interaction, so high-risk colors (red/orange/amber) are plausible

pi_items.verified_value: Cannot independently verify exact color without accessing app; however consistent with high-risk NSAID classification
pi_items.source: Cancer Drug Interactions system uses traffic light colors (red/amber/orange/yellow/green) to indicate severity. Diclofenac-methotrexate has documented fatal interactions, so high-risk colors (red/orange/amber) are plausible

pi_items.verified_value: Cannot independently verify exact color without accessing app; however consistent with lower-risk analgesic classification
pi_items.source: Cancer Drug Interactions system uses traffic light colors (red/amber/orange/yellow/green) to indicate severity. Paracetamol is relatively safe with methotrexate, so lower-risk colors (green/yellow) are plausible. Note: 'grey' and 'gray' are not standard traffic light colors in documented systems

privileged_info_summary: The task evaluates drug interactions for a patient on Methotrexate using the real Liverpool Cancer iChart app. All four drugs are real: Methotrexate (cancer/autoimmune drug), Ibuprofen (NSAID), Diclofenac (NSAID), and Paracetamol (analgesic). The safety classifications are clinically accurate: Ibuprofen and Diclofenac are unsafe with methotrexate due to reduced renal clearance causing methotrexate toxicity (with documented fatal cases), while Paracetamol is relatively safe (though with monitoring for hepatotoxicity). Paracetamol is correctly identified as the safest analgesic option for a methotrexate patient.
